summary: The neonate’s presentation—absent sacra/lumbar vertebrae, small pelvis, shallow intergluteal cleft, and club feet—is characteristic of Trisomy 18 (Edwards syndrome). This condition results from an extra copy of chromosome 18 and typically presents with multiple major structural anomalies including those seen here.
